query: What are the top AI news headlines today? Include sources.
textResponse:
Here are **top AI headlines for today (Thu, April 9, 2026)**, with sources. (Because “today” is time-sensitive, I pulled items published within the past few days where possible.)

1) **EU Commission opens call for experts for a “Frontier AI” forum (first meeting April 23, 2026)**
   - Source: European Commission — Shaping Europe’s Digital Future. ([digital-strategy.ec.europa.eu](https://digital-strategy.ec.europa.eu/en/news/commission-seeks-experts-forum-frontier-ai?utm_source=openai))

2) **OpenAI publishes a policy vision for the AI economy (e.g., public wealth funds, robot taxes, four-day workweek)**
   - Source: TechCrunch (Apr 6, 2026). ([techcrunch.com](https://techcrunch.com/2026/04/06/openais-vision-for-the-ai-economy-public-wealth-funds-robot-taxes-and-a-four-day-work-week/?utm_source=openai))  
   - Additional coverage: Yahoo/Tech (Apr 6, 2026). ([tech.yahoo.com](https://tech.yahoo.com/ai/articles/openai-touts-4-day-week-181924589.html?utm_source=openai))

3) **NVIDIA/AI chips + robotics: Serve Robotics unveils an AI conversational robot (“Maggie”) at NVIDIA GTC 2026**
   - Source: Simply Wall St (Apr 8, 2026). ([simplywall.st](https://simplywall.st/stocks/us/semiconductors/nasdaq-nvda/nvidia/news/this-week-in-ai-chips-ai-meets-robotics-revolutionizing-real?utm_source=openai))

4) **EU AI Act timeline clarification: full application date and staggered provisions**
   - Source: EU Commission FAQ (“Navigating the AI Act”) noting broad application **2 years after entry into force (Aug 2, 2026)** with exceptions. ([digital-strategy.ec.europa.eu](https://digital-strategy.ec.europa.eu/en/faqs/navigating-ai-act?utm_source=openai))  
   - Background on enforcement model: European Parliament Research Service (Mar 17, 2026). ([europarl.europa.eu](https://www.europarl.europa.eu/thinktank/en/document/EPRS_ATA%282026%29785670?utm_source=openai))

If you tell me whether you want **business/investing**, **policy/regulation**, or **research-only** headlines (and how many—e.g., top 10), I can refine the list to that slice of AI news.
